tickets.title: US001
tickets.description:
As an Academic Advisor, I want to manually override prerequisite restrictions for an eligible student within the system, so that I can instantly resolve exceptional enrollment cases with special permission.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Pass/Fail Check 1: Verify that the "Override Prerequisite" interface component is visible and interactable ONLY when logged in with authorized Advisor credentials.
- [ ] Pass/Fail Check 2: Verify that the system prevents the Advisor from saving the override if the

tickets.title: US002
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to drag and drop course cards from the catalog sidebar onto a visual calendar layout, so that I can intuitively assess whether the selected classes physically fit into my weekly schedule.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Pass/Fail Check 1: Verify that dragging a course and dropping it into an empty calendar slot automatically snaps the course to its exact scheduled timetabled hours.
- [ ] Pass/Fail Check 2: Verify that if a dropped course overlaps in time with an already planned

analysis.ambiguity1: The statement from the Head of Advising regarding "manual override if a student has special permission" is highly ambiguous. It fails to define what criteria, conditions, or physical documentation qualify as "special permission". Additionally, it does not specify whether this override requires a multi-step digital approval workflow or a direct privilege granted unconditionally to specific advisor roles.
analysis.ambiguity2: The freshman student request to "warn me if I am choosing a class that starts at 8:00 AM" lacks technical constraints and measurable metrics. The requirement is ambiguous because it does not state whether this should be a soft warning (a simple visual notification) or a hard restriction blocking the schedule entry. Furthermore, it is unclear if the 8:00 AM threshold is a hardcoded system-wide rule or a customizable user preference.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: A direct logical conflict exists between the Head of Advising (who wants students to plan 4 years ahead) and the Registrar (who insists that full classes cannot be added to the roadmap based on a live database connection). Real-time class capacity for future semesters (e.g., 2 or 3 years from now) does not exist yet.  

Resolution: Implement a "Dual-state Roadmap" strategy. Introduce a Planning Mode for long-term roadmaps (up to 4 years) where validation is solely based on curriculum course prerequisites, completely ignoring live capacity constraints. Switch to a Registration Mode exclusively for the upcoming active semester, which locks the roadmap into a live-sync state with the Registrar database, hard-blocking any additions to full classes.
analysis.nfr1:
Security (Data Privacy)
Data Isolation: Under no circumstances should any student account be granted permission or system access to view, search, or read another student's generated academic schedule or personal roadmap
analysis.nfr2:
Auditability / Maintainability (System Logging)
Audit Trail: The system must automatically and immutably log every event where an advisor executes a manual prerequisite override, capturing the advisor ID, student ID, timestamp, and the mandatory justification text.
analysis.nfr3:
Performance & Reliability (Database Integration)
Live Database Synchronization: The roadmap application must maintain a continuous, low-latency API connection with the university's central live Registrar database to guarantee up-to-the-minute structural course and capacity status verification.
